data.name: Label_DraftDetail
data.value: Your working copy until you save a named preset.

data.name: Check_CenterCharacter
data.value: Center character on screen (locks Horizontal Shift to 0)

data.name: Check_MountHeight
data.value: Match mount/horse height to player camera height

data.name: Check_Steadycam
data.value: Smooth camera transitions (eliminates zoom jitter between states)

data.name: Check_LockOnAutoRotate
data.value: Disable camera snap to target when locking on -- Credits to @sillib1980

data.name: Check_CenterHud
data.value: Center gameplay HUD within max width

data.name: Label_ImportModPackageDesc
data.value: JSON patch from JSON Mod Manager or CDUMM

data.name: Label_ImportUcmPresetDesc
data.value: UCM preset file from another user

data.name: Label_GodModeGoldModified
data.value: Gold = modified by UCM Quick/Fine Tune

data.name: Label_GodModeGreenSacred
data.value: Green = sacred (your God Mode edits, protected from rebuilds)

data.name: Status_ImportedAndSaved
data.value: Imported '{0}' and saved — hit Install to apply.

data.name: Status_AdvLoadedEditor
data.value: Loaded — edit values and click Apply to Game.

data.name: Status_ResetToUcmQuickDefaults
data.value: Reset to UCM Quick defaults plus vanilla.

data.name: Status_InstallFailed
data.value: Install failed. Is the game running?

data.name: Status_NoBackup
data.value: No backup found. Camera may already be vanilla.

data.name: Status_StaleBackup
data.value: Backup is from old version. Verify game files.

data.name: Status_NoBackupMayBeVanilla
data.value: No backup found. The game camera may already be vanilla.

data.name: Status_StaleBackupRefreshed
data.value: Game was updated since last install — backup has been refreshed. Verify game files on Steam, then install again.

data.name: Status_AdvCtrlMode
data.value: Advanced Controls — guided per-parameter camera tuning.

data.name: Status_ExpertMode
data.value: Expert mode — edit raw XML values and click Apply to Game.

data.name: Status_JsonMode
data.value: JSON Mod Manager — generate Crimson Desert Mod Manager v8 patch files.

data.name: Status_GeneratedPatches
data.value: Generated {0} patch regions. Click Save .json to export.

data.name: Status_JsonNeedsVanilla
data.value: JSON export needs vanilla camera files first.

data.name: Status_ResetVanilla
data.value: Reset all Advanced Controls to vanilla.

data.name: Status_AdvCtrlApplied
data.value: Applied {0} Advanced Control changes to game.

data.name: Status_CleanedOldData
data.value: Cleaned old v{0} data. Please click Install to apply your settings.

data.name: Status_GameFolderNotSetBrowse
data.value: Game folder not set. Click Install and you'll be prompted to browse.

data.name: Status_XboxReadOnly
data.value: Xbox/Game Pass: game folder is read-only. Move the game or fix folder permissions.

data.name: Status_UcmQuickMode
data.value: UCM Quick — broad camera shaping with previews and common controls.

data.name: Status_FineTuneMode
data.value: UCM Fine Tune — curated deeper tuning layered on top of UCM Quick.

data.name: Status_GodModeMode
data.value: God Mode — edit raw XML values and export them as JSON.

data.name: Status_SacredEdit
data.value: Sacred edit: this value is now protected from Quick/Fine Tune rebuilds.

data.name: Status_UnlockToReset
data.value: Unlock this preset in the sidebar to reset God Mode values.

data.name: Status_InstalledSession
data.value: Installed current session to game. Camera payload updated — {0} bytes ({1} compressed in PAZ).

data.name: Status_InstalledUnchanged
data.value: Install completed; camera entry in PAZ was unchanged ({0} bytes payload, {1} compressed).

data.name: Status_NoManifestFound
data.value: No manifest.json found in that folder.

data.name: Status_NoCameraXmlInPackage
data.value: No playercamerapreset.xml found in the mod package.

data.name: Status_NoSessionXmlInPreset
data.value: This file doesn't appear to be a UCM preset (no session_xml found).

data.name: Status_NoCommunityPresets
data.value:
No community presets available yet.
Check back later or contribute your own!

data.name: Status_ReadyToSave
data.value: Ready to save. Click Save when ready.

data.name: Status_NoSessionXmlAvailable
data.value: No session XML available. Edit settings or load a preset first.

data.name: Status_LoadedIntoGodMode
data.value: Loaded imported preset '{0}' into God Mode.

data.name: Status_BackupRefreshed
data.value: Camera backup was refreshed from your current game files. Use Install when you are ready to re-apply your preset.

data.name: Status_BackupRefreshFailed
data.value: Could not refresh camera backup automatically. If the camera is still modded, verify game files in Steam and restart UCM.

data.name: Status_ImportedNotRebuilt
data.value: Imported only - not yet rebuilt for this game.

data.name: Status_ImportedReadyNoGameFolder
data.value: Saved preset ready - set a game folder to check rebuild status.

data.name: Status_ImportedReadyRebuilt
data.value: Ready - already rebuilt for this game version.

data.name: Status_ImportedNeedsRebuild
data.value: Needs rebuild - current game version differs from the last build.

data.name: Msg_WrongFolder
data.value: That folder doesn't contain 0010\0.paz. Make sure you selected the correct Crimson Desert directory.

data.name: Msg_SetGameFolder
data.value: Set a game folder first (click Install to browse).

data.name: Msg_OverwritePreset
data.value: Overwrite preset '{0}' with current slider values?

data.name: Msg_OverwriteAdvPreset
data.value: Overwrite advanced preset '{0}' with current values?

data.name: Msg_XboxReadOnly
data.value:
Xbox / Game Pass game folder appears to be read-only.
To fix this, try one of: 1. Xbox App → Crimson Desert → Manage → Move to a different drive 2. Right-click the game folder → Properties → uncheck "Read-only" → Apply to all subfolders

data.name: Msg_ConfirmImportXml
data.value:
Install '{0}' directly to the game?
This will overwrite your current camera settings.

data.name: Msg_CannotExportJson
data.value:
The camera data in your game folder does not match UCM's vanilla backup.
JSON patches for external mod managers must use vanilla "original" bytes. Verify the game in Steam (or revert camera changes in your mod manager), then try again.

data.name: Msg_CrashError
Msg_OverwriteExists
data.value: A preset with this name already exists. Overwrite?

data.name: Msg_ConfirmDelete
data.value: Are you sure? This cannot be undone.

data.name: Msg_PresetUpdateAvailable
data.value:
An update is available for '{0}'.
Would you like to save a copy of the current version to My Presets before updating?

data.name: Msg_RawXmlImportNotice
data.value:
This preset was imported as raw XML and is not managed by UCM.
UCM Quick and Fine Tune use UCM's camera rule system which would override values from the imported mod. To protect your import, only God Mode editing is available.
To use UCM features like Steadycam and FoV control, create a new UCM preset from the sidebar instead.

data.name: Msg_FineTuneGodModeNeedGameFolder
data.value: Fine Tune and God Mode need your game folder to read camera data. Set your game folder first.

data.name: Msg_UcmPresetProtected
data.value:
UCM presets are protected. {0} changes could corrupt the preset's carefully tuned values.
Duplicate this preset first to create your own editable copy, then use Fine Tune or God Mode freely.
Open {0} in read-only mode anyway? (You can browse values but changes won't save.)

data.name: Msg_GameFilesModifiedBody
data.value:
Your game camera files have been modified by UCM v2.x, another camera mod, or a mod manager. UCM needs the original vanilla files to create a clean backup.
Click the button below to remove the modified file, then verify on Steam to re-download the original.

data.name: Msg_PazRemovedInstructions
data.value: 0.paz deleted. Now: 1. Close UCM 2. Steam → Crimson Desert → Properties → Installed Files → "Verify integrity of game files" 3. Relaunch UCM after verification completes

data.name: Msg_NoPamtFound
data.value: No matching 0.pamt was found beside the selected 0.paz. Set your game folder so v3 can use the current 0010\0.pamt as the archive index.

data.name: Msg_GameFolderNotSetForImport
data.value: Game folder not set. v3 needs the current game to rebuild this preset safely.

data.name: Msg_JsonExportNeedsVanilla
data.value: JSON export needs vanilla camera files (verify game, then try again).

data.name: Msg_InstallCompletedUnexpected
data.value: Install completed but returned an unexpected result. Camera may not have been applied correctly — try launching the game to verify.

data.name: Msg_RestoreCompletedUnconfirmed
data.value: Restore completed but the result could not be confirmed. Launch the game to check if the camera is back to vanilla.

data.name: Tip_OpenGameFolder
data.value: Open game folder (or browse to set it manually)

data.name: Tip_Steadycam
data.value: Smooths blending, damping and velocity offsets between movement states. Disable to restore vanilla camera transitions.

data.name: Tip_Kofi
data.value: Opens Ko-fi — tips help keep UCM going

data.name: Tip_ExportXml
data.value: Save the current live camera XML to a file

data.name: Tip_ImportXml
data.value: Install a camera XML file directly to the game

data.name: Tip_LoadFromSimple
data.value: Populate all sliders from your current Simple mode settings

data.name: Tip_ResetVanilla
data.value: Reset all sliders to vanilla XML values

data.name: Tip_ImportXmlFile
data.value: Load values from a playercamerapreset.xml file

data.name: Tip_GenerateCurrent
data.value: Uses whatever is currently configured in Simple mode

data.name: Tip_GenerateFromXml
data.value: Pick a playercamerapreset.xml file to diff against vanilla

data.name: Tip_ImportPreset
data.value: Import a camera XML or PAZ archive as a preset

data.name: Tip_ExportPreset
data.value: Share as JSON (Mod Manager), session XML, or patched 0.paz — uses your current UCM session.

data.name: Tip_RestorePreset
data.value: Restore the original camera chunk from UCM's backup.

data.name: Tip_InstallToGame
data.value: Apply the current UCM session directly to the game files.

data.name: Tip_ImportModPackage
data.value: Select a folder containing manifest.json — imports name, author, and camera XML automatically

data.name: Tip_ImportPaz
data.value: Select a 0.paz file — UCM will extract the camera XML from it

data.name: Tip_ImportUcmPreset
data.value: Import a .ucmpreset file shared by another UCM user

data.name: Tip_Snooze7d
data.value: Hide this reminder for one week

data.name: Tip_UpdateAvailable
data.value: Update available — click to download latest version

data.name: Tip_FovCombo
data.value: Adds this many degrees to each Fov and InDoorFov in the camera file. Indoor and outdoor rows start from different vanilla numbers, so God Mode will show many different final values—not one angle everywhere.

data.name: Tip_CenteredCamera
data.value: Separate from Horizontal shift ~0.5: that value is often vanilla lateral bias in the XML. This option forces shift to 0 and applies stronger centering rules (RightOffset toward 0 where supported).

data.name: Tip_LockOnAutoRotate
data.value: When checked, the camera stays in place instead of snapping around to face the locked-on target.

data.name: Help_ShiftExplain
data.value: How far left/right the camera sits from the character. Centered camera locks this to 0.

data.name: Help_ShiftCenteredOn
data.value: Centered Camera forces character to screen center — untick to adjust

data.name: Help_ShiftCenteredOff
data.value: 0 = vanilla (character slightly left). Center is ~0.5. Negative = further left, positive = further right.

data.name: Help_HShiftTipUnlocked
data.value: Left/right framing as a delta on RightOffset (on foot, mounts, aim, and related rows). 0 matches vanilla side bias. Increase toward ~0.5 to pull toward geometric center in the file; negative values bias further left. Matches the top-down FoV preview. Centered camera below is separate: it locks this slider to 0 and applies stronger centering.

data.name: Help_Limits
data.value: Limits: Dist 1.5-12.0 | Height -1.6 to 0.5 | Shift -3.0 to 3.0

data.name: Help_PreviewShiftExplain
data.value: Shift 0 = vanilla (char left) | 0.5 = center | negative = more left | positive = more right

data.name: Help_GameUpdateNote
data.value: Game updates will overwrite modded files. After each Crimson Desert update, re-run UCM Install to restore your camera settings.

data.name: Help_AdvApplyInfo
data.value: Each slider shows the current value and the vanilla default (dimmed). Click ↺ on any row to reset that parameter to vanilla. Hit Apply to write all changes to the game.

data.name: Help_AdvChangesInfo
data.value: Changes are applied on top of vanilla XML — they do not interact with Simple mode presets. Use "Load from Simple" to start from your current preset.

data.name: Help_OnFootZoomDesc
data.value: The game has 3 scroll-wheel zoom levels on foot. ZL2 is closest (default view), ZL3 is mid-range, ZL4 is furthest out. Each level independently controls camera distance, height, and horizontal position. These apply to all on-foot states: idle, walking, running, sprinting, and combat movement.

data.name: Help_HorseZoomDesc
data.value: Horse cameras use the 2 vanilla zoom levels (ZL2-ZL3). All 8 horse states are synchronized so the camera doesn't jolt during speed changes while keeping the real game zoom ladder.

data.name: Help_CombatDesc
data.value: When you lock onto an enemy the camera switches to combat tracking mode. TargetRate controls how aggressively the camera follows the enemy — lower values give you more manual control. ScreenClampRate controls how centered the enemy stays on screen.

data.name: Help_SmoothingDesc
data.value: These control how the camera transitions between movement states. BlendInTime is how many seconds the camera takes to ease into a state. OffsetLength controls camera sway — setting it to 0 gives a steadycam feel with no sideways movement.

data.name: Help_AimingDesc
data.value: When you aim with the lantern, bow, or weapon zoom the camera shifts to position the crosshair. These RightOffset values scale proportionally from your horizontal shift so the camera doesn't snap sideways when activating abilities.

data.name: Help_JsonHowItWorks
data.value: This tab generates a .json patch file compatible with Crimson Desert JSON Mod Manager v8. Drop the file into the mod manager's Mods/ folder and your camera settings will appear as a toggleable mod — no UCM required.

data.name: Help_JsonDiffExplain
data.value: The patch works by comparing your modified camera file against the vanilla file byte-by-byte and recording only the differences. This means it's game-version specific — if the game updates, regenerate the patch.

data.name: Help_JsonStep1Desc
data.value: This info appears inside the mod manager so people know what the mod is.

data.name: Help_JsonStep2Desc
data.value: Choose what settings to patch from. Current Settings uses whatever is active in Simple mode right now. From XML File lets you pick any exported playercamerapreset.xml to patch from instead.

data.name: Help_JsonStep3Desc
data.value: Save the .json file, then drop it into the Mods/ folder inside your Crimson Desert JSON Mod Manager installation. It will appear as a toggleable mod.

data.name: Help_UcmQuickIntro
data.value: This is the fast, curated UCM layer: broad camera shaping, previews, and the most common camera choices.

data.name: Help_FineTuneIntro
data.value: UCM Fine Tune is the curated deep-tuning layer. It starts from your UCM Quick setup and lets you refine the fields UCM intentionally exposes.

data.name: Help_FineTuneGodModeNote
data.value: God Mode lists every row in the file. Sliders here edit specific attributes—those numbers should match the same attribute in God Mode. Other rows still change from Quick (FoV boost, style, combat, steadycam, etc.) or vanilla, so do not expect one slider to explain the whole grid.

data.name: Help_GodModeIntro
data.value: God Mode exposes the raw camera rows. Use it when UCM Quick is not enough and you want to jump straight into full direct control, or after UCM Fine Tune if you want to go even deeper.

data.name: Help_GodModeSessionNote
data.value: Values here are the merged session: Quick + Fine Tune + any edits in this grid. FoV in Quick is a delta added per row (Fov and InDoorFov), so angles differ by state. Fine Tune matches here row-for-row only for the attributes those sliders control.

data.name: Help_GlobalFovDesc
data.value: These rows cover broader FoV normalization and shared camera systems that apply outside the basic on-foot and horse groups. Use them when you want UCM Fine Tune to reflect the full mod surface, including lock-on families, warmachine states, and swim cameras.

data.name: Help_SpecialMountsDesc
data.value: Elephant, wyvern, canoe, warmachine, broom, and traversal-adjacent camera families all have their own zoom behavior. These controls expose the extra mount values the mod already changes behind the scenes.

data.name: Help_LockOnZoomDesc
data.value: Adjusts camera distance when locking onto or guarding a target, relative to your on-foot distance. 0% is seamless with Steadycam. Negative values zoom in for a cinematic focus effect; positive values pull back for more battlefield awareness.

data.name: Help_CenterHudDesc
data.value: Centers gameplay HUD elements within the specified max width (pixels). Useful for ultrawide displays where HUD elements are spread too far apart. Only affects gameplay HUD, not menus. Credits to wsres.

data.name: Help_CommunityBrowserSubtitle
data.value: Browse camera presets shared by the UCM community. Downloaded presets appear in your sidebar.

data.name: Help_UcmBrowserSubtitle
data.value: Browse and download official UCM camera presets. Downloaded presets appear in your sidebar.

data.name: Help_ManagedByUcmDesc
data.value: UCM manages over 150 camera states behind the scenes — FoV consistency across combat, guard, mount, and lock-on; smooth transitions between every state; distance scaling so lock-on doesn't snap inward. Quick sliders and Fine Tune build on top of these rules safely. God Mode lets you override individual values when you need to.

data.name: Help_FullManualDesc
data.value: Starts from the game's vanilla camera XML with no UCM rules applied. UCM Quick and Fine Tune are disabled because their sliders would overwrite your manual edits — UCM modifies hundreds of values across combat, mount, lock-on, and smoothing states, which would conflict with hand-tuned values. Only God Mode is available for direct XML editing. Use this when importing from another camera mod or when you need full control over every value.

data.name: Help_ImportedPresetsNote
data.value: Imports are saved in the import_presets folder next to this app and listed under Imported in the sidebar.

data.name: Help_ChooseImportSource
data.value: Choose what type of file you want to import.

data.name: Help_PasteAdvancedImport
data.value: Paste the UCM_ADV: string to import advanced settings.

data.name: Help_UnsavedSession
data.value: Start from UCM Quick, load something from Preset Manager, or import a creator preset.

data.name: Dlg_CopyPasteHint
data.value: Copy and paste on NexusMods, Discord, etc.

data.name: Dlg_PasteAdvancedBelow
data.value: Paste an advanced settings string below:

data.name: Dlg_PasteAdvancedHint
data.value: Must start with UCM_ADV: (from Advanced Export)

data.name: Dlg_NotAdvString
data.value: Not an advanced settings string (must start with UCM_ADV:)

data.name: Dlg_EnterPresetName
data.value: Enter a name for this preset:

data.name: Dlg_EnterImportedPresetName
data.value: Enter a name for this imported preset:

data.name: Dlg_BrowseGameFolder
data.value: Select Crimson Desert folder (contains the '0010' folder)

data.name: Dlg_ImportCameraXml
data.value: Import Camera XML — installs directly to game

data.name: GamePath_NotSet
data.value: Game folder: NOT SET (click Install to browse)

data.name: Help_ExportJsonFormat
data.value: Exports a byte-patch .json you can import into JSON Mod Manager or Crimson Desert Ultimate Mods Manager. Use whichever mod manager you prefer — recipients do not need UCM.

data.name: Help_ExportXmlFormat
data.value: Exports playercamerapreset.xml with the same normalization UCM uses when installing (UTF-8 BOM, comments stripped). Great for Nexus text uploads, hand editing, or re-import in UCM. Whoever installs it still needs matching compressed entry sizes for their game build.

data.name: Help_ExportPazFormat
data.value: Nexus-style drop-in archive: a copy of your game's 0010/0.paz with only the camera data updated to match your current UCM session — players who don't use a JSON mod manager can replace one file. It only works for the same game patch as your install (same archive layout); say that on the mod page. Tell downloaders to back up vanilla 0010/0.paz before swapping.

data.name: Help_ExportUcmPresetFormat
data.value: Exports your current session as a .ucmpreset file that other UCM users can drop into their presets folder or share via the community catalog. Contains your full camera configuration including all Quick, Fine Tune, and God Mode settings.

data.name: Help_ExportJsonStep2
data.value: Uses your live UCM session (Quick, Fine Tune, or God Mode). Pick a preset in the sidebar or use Import if you are starting from XML or a PAZ on disk.

data.name: Help_ExportXmlStep2
data.value: Prepare checks that your session encodes for this game folder, then you save the XML file.

data.name: Help_ExportPazStep2
data.value: Prepare verifies encoding against your game folder. Save writes a full patched 0.paz (large file). Session source is the same as the other formats — sidebar preset or Import first if needed.

data.name: Help_ExportPresetStep2
data.value: No encoding needed — saves your session directly. Fill in the info fields above so others know what they're getting.

data.name: Help_XmlEncodingMatches
data.value: XML encoding matches this install's camera entry. Save as playercamerapreset.xml for sharing or editing.

data.name: Help_PazUploadNote
data.value: Upload size will match a full 0.paz. Recipients replace ...\0010\0.paz after backing up vanilla. Only share with players on the same game version as this export.

data.name: Help_ExportModInfoNote
data.value: JSON uses these fields in modinfo. XML uses Title as the default save name. 0.paz defaults to 0.paz so you can drop it into 0010 without renaming.

data.name: Help_GeneratedPatchReady
data.value: Generated {0} patch regions. Save when ready.

data.name: Help_PazImportErrorDetail
data.value:
This usually means the PAZ file is from a different game version than your current install. The archive index (0.pamt) doesn't match the PAZ file structure.
TO FIX: Place the matching 0.pamt file in the same folder as the 0.paz you're importing. Both files must be from the same game version.
